mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, consumer confidence and wage growth expectations accelerate, driving retail spending and corporate revenue surprises that lift equity prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and residential construction spending signals robust aggregate demand expansion, which increases corporate cash flow expectations and drives positive index returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production increase exceeding 0.5 percent indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which expand profit margins and push equity valuations higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant difference between triggered and non-triggered periods (bootstrap p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2.0 percent sustains corporate earnings momentum, which mechanically supports equity price appreciation over a six-month forward window.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where real GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings implying an annualized inflation rate above 2.5 percent force the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, yielding negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward information coefficient remains positive or neutral across all 3-year rolling windows, indicating inflation readings do not predict negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts household disposable income and consumption, driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward SPX returns against both series.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5 percent alongside a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals an aggregate demand shock that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and triggers negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where both GDP growth and housing starts exhibit the specified weakness.
